_doc: Triaged npm-audit advisories. scripts/audit-gate.mjs fails the build for any high/critical advisory NOT listed here (each needs a reason + expiry), and for any listed entry past its expiry, so triage decisions cannot rot silently. The postcss advisories are resolved at source via an overrides pin in package.json (postcss ^8.5.12), not triaged here.

allow.reason: Not reachable in this deployment. The frontend is a pure static export (next.config.mjs output:'export') served as plain HTML/JS/CSS - it ships NO Next.js server runtime. Every one of these advisories targets server-side machinery this build never emits: middleware/proxy bypass (no middleware), Server Actions DoS/SSRF (static export cannot run Server Actions), custom-server SSRF, response-body cache confusion (no server cache), unbounded Edge-runtime payloads (no edge runtime), rewrite-destination SSRF (no runtime rewrites), /_next/image optimizer DoS (image optimizer is disabled under output:'export'), and internal Server Function endpoint disclosure (no server functions). No stable patched next exists yet (latest is 16.2.11, still inside the advisory range; the fix lands in 16.3.x) - re-triage when it ships.
